heading: Absence of certain police and firemen
label: 6324 Absence of certain police and firemen
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large .5 U.S.C. 2071, , .Aug. 21, 1964Pub. L. 88–471, § 578 Stat. 583The word “officer” is omitted as covered by “member”.In subsection (b), the words “injury or illness” are substituted for “injury or disease” to conform to subsection (a).Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

children.label: a
children.text: Sick leave may not be charged to the account of a member of the Metropolitan Police force or the Fire Department of the District of Columbia, the United States Park Police force, or the United States Secret Service Uniformed Division for an absence due to injury or illness resulting from the performance of duty.
the District of Columbia Council for members of the Metropolitan Police force and the Fire Department of the District of Columbia;
the Secretary of the Interior for the United States Park Police force; and
the Secretary of Homeland Security for the United States Secret Service Uniformed Division.

children.label: c
children.text: This section shall not apply to members of the United States Secret Service Uniformed Division who are covered under chapter 84 for the purpose of retirement benefits.
